verses.text: God stands among the rulers and judges, asking, 'How long will ye judge unjustly?' He holds earthly authorities to account. Those who wield power do so under the eye of the supreme Judge.
God stands among the rulers and judges, asking, 'How long will ye judge unjustly?' He holds earthly authorities to account. Those who wield power do so under the eye of the supreme Judge.

He charges them to 'defend the poor and fatherless' and rescue the needy from the wicked. Justice means protecting the weak. The office of the judge is to be a shield to those who cannot defend themselves.

He charges them to 'defend the poor and fatherless' and rescue the needy from the wicked. Justice means protecting the weak. The office of the judge is to be a shield to those who cannot defend themselves.

But they walk in darkness, knowing nothing, so the foundations are shaken; though called 'gods,' they shall 'die like men.' Their authority will not save them. Every ruler, however high, must answer to God and fall like any mortal.

But they walk in darkness, knowing nothing, so the foundations are shaken; though called 'gods,' they shall 'die like men.' Their authority will not save them. Every ruler, however high, must answer to God and fall like any mortal.

But they walk in darkness, knowing nothing, so the foundations are shaken; though called 'gods,' they shall 'die like men.' Their authority will not save them. Every ruler, however high, must answer to God and fall like any mortal.
'Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shalt inherit all nations.' The psalm ends looking to God. When human justice fails, the cry of the oppressed turns to the Judge of all.
